text.title: Sidekiq Background Jobs Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every Sidekiq Background Jobs application MUST enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. Includes a `skip-this-methodology` rule so the decision tree can terminate cleanly.

rule.statement: Apply Sidekiq Background Jobs only to a single instance per call. Never batch multiple unrelated decisions through one pass.
rule.rationale: Batching turns a 1-decision artefact into a debate club. Each instance gets its own record so the rationale is traceable.

rule.statement: Every input field has a declared type and source. Free-form prose without a source citation is rejected at the input gate.
rule.rationale: Untyped inputs let LLMs hallucinate plausible-but-wrong values. Typed + sourced inputs make the output falsifiable.

rule.statement: The output names a single accountable owner (handle, email, or named org role). No 'team' or 'we' as owner.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; future readers need a named human to chase.

rule.statement: Output carries a semver-style version and last_reviewed date. Stale records (>90 days, no review) are flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Methodology outputs decay; treating them as immutable is a defect. Versioning + review-by-date forces refresh cadence.

rule.statement: Every section of the artefact cites the specific gap it closes — by input name, by quoted requirement, or by linked prior artefact.
rule.rationale: Without grounding, the artefact becomes generic boilerplate. Citation makes the claim falsifiable.

rule.statement: Skip when any Skip-If condition in AGENTS.md holds. Do NOT produce an artefact; surface the gap to the requester.
rule.rationale: A methodology applied outside its scope produces low-signal artefacts that pollute the artefact store; the decision tree needs a clean terminator.
